title: Debate in Delhi on Trump, Trust, and Strategic Choices
gist: Trump's second-term posture toward India has shocked New Delhi, with warmth fading even before inauguration; India's strategic community is debating whether the cooling is transient or a fundamental rupture that could affect India's growth story.

insights.claim: Donald Trump's posture toward India in his second term has shocked and awed New Delhi, reversing the prior warmth in the US-India relationship.
insights.speaker: Muqtedar Khan
insights.so_what: A durable US-India cooling would reprice the strategic premium built into India's China-hedge narrative.

insights.claim: Trump extended an unusually early invitation to Chinese President Xi Jinping to his inauguration while Modi received no comparable invitation, signaling a shift even before Trump took office.
insights.speaker: Muqtedar Khan
insights.so_what: Symbolic snubs foreshadowed a substantive reordering of US priorities toward Beijing over New Delhi.
insights.locator: There was a noticeable shift even before his inauguration

insights.claim: The key question in New Delhi is whether the US-India shift is transient or a fundamental rupture with long-term impacts on India's growth story.
insights.speaker: Muqtedar Khan
insights.so_what: How this binary resolves determines whether investors treat the cooling as noise or a regime change in India's external tailwinds.
insights.locator: The key question animating New Delhi
